subject: fix(scheduling): UI, a11y, and mobile UX from review findings
body:
DateTimePicker split into platform-specific files via Metro's file-extension
resolution:
  DateTimePicker.tsx         - web (<input type="datetime-local">)
  DateTimePicker.native.tsx  - iOS (combined spinner) + Android (chained flow)

- L6: No more render-time require() + eslint-disable; each platform's module
  is imported at module scope.

- H7: Android picker rewritten as an explicit chain — tap opens date mode,
  confirm auto-advances to time mode, confirm closes. Symmetric "Change date"
  / "Change time" buttons make both steps discoverable. iOS uses a single
  combined mode="datetime" spinner for a simpler flow.

ScheduleComposerModal:
  - M9: leadOk and min/max bounds recompute on a 15s tick while the modal is
    open so stale picks are caught even if the user hesitates.
  - Error banner clears automatically when the user picks a new time.
  - useId() + real HTML <label htmlFor> on web ties the datetime input to its
    visible label (was only a separate Text node).
  - accessibilityRole / accessibilityLabel on close button, confirm button,
    and error region (accessibilityLiveRegion='polite', role='alert').
  - friendlyScheduleError() maps thrown errors to user copy.

ScheduledMessagesModal:
  - M3: Cancel flow confirms first via showConfirmDialog (destructive, no undo).
  - L8: "Cancel" -> "Cancel send" on pending rows to distinguish from the
    modal's own Cancel dismissal. Failed rows get "Dismiss" (softer).
  - M8: Row-level accessibilityLabel announces the scheduled time;
    Reschedule/Cancel/Dismiss buttons carry context in their a11y labels.
  - New realtimeDegraded banner when the hook reports a dropped subscription.
  - Empty state expanded with a "Tap the clock icon on the compose bar" hint.
  - dismissScheduledMessage wired for failed rows (soft-cancel under the hood).

ComposeBar:
  - Clock icon gains accessibilityRole='button', accessibilityLabel, and
    accessibilityHint explaining the interaction.
  - Kept always-visible-but-dim (rather than hidden-when-empty) for
    discoverability — first-time users need to see the affordance exists.

Tutorial:
  - L10: "Manage pending ones from the chat menu" -> "Tap the chat menu to
    cancel or reschedule" — more concrete.

Tags: scheduling, ui, a11y, mobile, ux, forms
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: message-scheduling
Semver: patch
